name: underlying one-step method
statement_text:
Corresponding to a linear multistep method [α, β], the
member of G1 represents the ‘underlying one-step method’.
  As we have already remarked, the mapping Φ in (422b), if it exists in more
than a notional sense, is really the object of interest and this really is the
underlying one-step method.

variables.name: G1
variables.definition: the group of mappings from trees to real numbers, representing the underlying one-step method

variables.name: [α, β]
variables.definition: a linear multistep method with coefficients α and β

variables.name: η
variables.definition: a mapping from trees to real numbers, representing the underlying one-step method in G1

variables.name: r(t)
variables.definition: the order (or rank) of a tree t

variables.name: D
variables.definition: an operator on trees, likely related to differentiation or tree operations

variables.name: Φ
variables.definition: the mapping representing the underlying one-step method, related to η

equations.content: Not fully given in the text, but referenced as the mapping Φ that represents the underlying one-step method.

preamble: The preceding text discusses the existence of a mapping η in the group G1 that satisfies equation (422a) for a preconsistent and stable linear multistep method [α, β]. This mapping is constructed inductively on the order of trees, and it represents the underlying one-step method of the multistep method.
